interface: core v1 ConfigMap via server-side apply (stable since v1.22 / apps GA); researched against v1.36 documentation and the official OpenAPI specification

auth.redirect_rule: the bearer token must never be sent to a different origin; any 3xx surfaces as KubeApiError(reason=Redirect, code=<status>) instead of being followed

missing.client_rule: read as null, not an error

params.force: appended as force=true only when conflicts should be re-acquired; omitted otherwise

apply.query_encoding: path segments and query values are percent-encoded per RFC 3986 (space is %20, not +; '/' in a name arrives as %2F)
apply.body: a partial ConfigMap carrying only the fields this manager owns: apiVersion v1, kind ConfigMap, metadata.name/namespace (+labels), data; managedFields must never be sent

success.200: the object existed and was configured
success.201: apply created the object; no separate POST is needed

yaml_projection.key_order: metadata.labels and data keys are serialized in lexicographic order
yaml_projection.scalar_quoting: label and data values are always double-quoted with JSON-style escaping so YAML cannot retype them ('true', '007', 'a: b', newlines)
yaml_projection.data_key_charset: data keys must match [A-Za-z0-9._-]+ (API server validation); anything else is rejected client-side
yaml_projection.empty_data: an empty data map serializes as 'data: {}' so the manager still owns data

conflict.details: Status.details.causes[]: {reason: FieldManagerConflict, field: <path like .data.retention>, message: 'conflict with "<manager>"'}
conflict.client_rule: decode causes into {field, manager}[]; without force the reconciler reports action=conflict and never retries silently; force=true re-acquires the fields

reconcile_policy.unchanged: skip the PATCH entirely when every desired data key and every desired label already has an identical value on the live object; foreign extra keys are irrelevant

errors.client_rule: non-2xx responses decode into KubeApiError{code, reason, message}
